title: US strikes Iran, Tehran hits Gulf states, says Strait of Hormuz closed
gist: The US-Iran ceasefire collapsed as both sides traded heavy strikes, Iran again closed the Strait of Hormuz (a fifth of world oil/LNG shipments) and hit US facilities across the Gulf, sending energy prices surging and fueling global inflation with US gasoline prices a political risk for Trump ahead of November elections.

insights.claim: The Strait of Hormuz, which carried one-fifth of the world's oil and LNG shipments before the war, was declared closed by Iran until 'the end of U.S. interference in this region.'
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: A quantified choke on ~20% of seaborne oil/LNG is a first-order catalyst for energy prices.
insights.locator: U.S. Central Command, however, said commercial vessels

insights.claim: Iran's effective blockade of the strait has caused energy prices to surge, fuelling global inflation.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Directly links the conflict to a global inflation impulse — reprice rate-cut expectations.
insights.locator: The war has destabilized the Gulf

insights.claim: Higher gasoline prices are politically sensitive for Trump ahead of November congressional elections.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Domestic political pressure could force US moves to reopen the strait or de-escalate.
insights.locator: The war has destabilized the Gulf

insights.claim: The US revoked the license authorizing the sale of Iranian crude on Tuesday after Qatari and Saudi commercial tankers came under fire.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Removes sanctioned Iranian barrels from legal markets, tightening supply further.

insights.claim: US Central Command said US forces hit 140 Iranian military targets Saturday, out of more than 300 across three nights of strikes aimed at degrading Iran's ability to attack shipping.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: The scale (300+ targets) points to a sustained air campaign, not a limited reprisal.

insights.claim: Iran expanded its targets — command centers and drone hangars in Jordan, a US radar site in Kuwait, carrier support/refueling platforms in Oman, and a jet maintenance and command facility in Qatar where three including a child were injured.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Iran's sharp escalation in pace and geographic breadth raises spillover risk across the Gulf.

insights.claim: Iran's Sunday strike on Qatar broke a pattern of avoiding Qatar since early April and the UAE since early May, marking a deliberate escalation.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Breaking prior restraint signals Iran is willing to widen the war's footprint.
insights.locator: In recent weeks, Iran had hit Kuwait

insights.claim: The attack on Qatar undermines Doha's central mediation role, which has previously said it would not act as a mediator so long as it was under attack.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Neutralizing the key mediator lengthens the path to any ceasefire.
insights.locator: Sunday's attack on Qatar targeted a state

insights.claim: Analysts say Tehran uses ship attacks, which it does not claim, to gain leverage in negotiations rather than as pure military acts.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Frames maritime attacks as bargaining tools — watch for concessions timed to strike pauses.
insights.locator: While Iran has not claimed responsibility

insights.claim: Iran's top negotiator Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf declared 'The era of one-sided deals is OVER,' casting doubt on last month's interim US-Iran agreement.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Signals Tehran will not honor the interim deal on prior terms — deal-collapse risk is high.
insights.quote: The era of one-sided deals is OVER. We told you: keep your word or pay the price. Reality is knocking.
insights.locator: 'KEEP YOUR WORD OR PAY THE PRICE'

insights.claim: Iran's new supreme leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, who has not been seen in public since the war began, threatened vengeance for his predecessor father's death.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: An unseen, vengeance-focused new leader adds uncertainty to Iran's decision-making.
insights.locator: A written statement from Iran's new supreme leader
